nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braunche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Marcel.

French: topographic name or nickname from Old French branche ‘branch’ (from Late Latin branca ‘foot, paw’), of uncertain application (compare German Zweig ); or a habitational name from (La) Branche, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare also Labranche .
English: variant of   Branch  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Zweig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Isadore, Yochanan, Akiva, Arie, Aron, Elchanan, Hyman, Moishe, Moshe, Shimon.  German Gerd, Hans, Manfred, Reinhold, Arnulf, Herta, Kurt.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German zwīg ‘branch, bough, twig, graft’, German Zweig, applied perhaps as a nickname for a new member in a family, offspring or as an occupational name for a horticultural expert. The Jewish surname is artificial. Compare Branch .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Braunche IV was born in 1170, in Gresham, Norfolk, England, his father, Richard de Braunche III, was 30 and his mother, Mrs Richard de Braunche III, was 29. He married wife Richard De Braunche IV before 1200, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Braunche IV est né en 1170, à Gresham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Braunche III, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs Richard de Braunche III, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé wife Richard De Braunche IV avant 1200, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Braunche IV 1170 in Gresham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Braunche III 30 und seine Mutter Mrs Richard de Braunche III 29. Er hat vor 1200 in Europa wife Richard De Braunche IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Braunche IV è nato il nel 1170 nel Gresham, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Richard de Braunche III, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Richard de Braunche III, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato wife Richard De Braunche IV prima del 1200, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Braunche IVが1170年にイギリスノーフォルクグレシャムで生まれた時，父Richard de Braunche IIIは30歳，母Mrs Richard de Braunche IIIは29歳であった。1200年以前にヨーロッパでwife Richard De Braunche IVと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Braunche IV(이)가 1170년 영국 노르폴크 그레샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Braunche III(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Richard de Braunche III(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1200년 이전 우럽에서 wife Richard De Braunche IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Braunche IV nasceu em 1170, em Gresham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Braunche III, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Richard de Braunche III, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com wife Richard De Braunche IV antes 1200, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Braunche IV родился в 1170 г. в местности грешам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Braunche III было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Richard de Braunche III, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени wife Richard De Braunche IV перед 1200 г. в местности Европа. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Braunche IV nació en 1170, en Gresham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Braunche III, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Mrs Richard de Braunche III, tenía 29 años. Se casó con wife Richard De Braunche IV antes de 1200, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en su pueblo.
